new_lines: // WorkspaceState represents the lifecycle state of a workspace.
// StateCreating means bootstrap is in progress. Operations block until Ready.
// StateReady means bootstrap completed. All operations proceed normally.
// StateFailed means bootstrap failed. The error is stored in stateErr.
// Can transition back to StateCreating on retry via CreateWorkspaceForBootstrap.
// credentialURLPattern matches URLs with embedded credentials (e.g., https://user:pass@host/...).
// redactCredentials replaces credentials in URLs within an error string.
// pathInfo maps an absolute path to its workspace and repo.
// Workspace represents a workspace containing multiple repositories.
RootPath    string // Base path for workspace (e.g., /workspaces/default)
Env         map[string]string             // per-workspace env defaults (from workspace/create)
stateErr     error         // non-nil only in StateFailed; the bootstrap error
ready        chan struct{} // closed when State transitions out of Creating
bootstrapped bool          // true after successful bootstrap via FinishBootstrap
// ManagedRepo represents a git repository within a workspace.
RelativePath string // Relative to workspace root
UpstreamURL  string // Upstream remote URL; clone fallback when origin doesn't exist
deleted      bool // Set by DeleteWorkspace; checked by Save under mr.mu.
// Per-repo lock and infrastructure (injected at creation time)
// runGitFn overrides git execution for testing. When non-nil, runGitIn
// calls this instead of exec'ing git. Production code leaves it nil.

// cloneWithRetry clones a repo from url with retry logic and empty-repo handling.
// Returns errRepoNotFound (wrapped) when the remote repo does not exist,
// allowing the caller to decide whether to fall back to an upstream URL.
func (m *WorkspaceManager) cloneWithRetry(mr *ManagedRepo, cloneURL string, parentDir string) error {
_, stderr, err = mr.runGitIn(ctx, parentDir, "clone", cloneURL, mr.AbsolutePath)
return fmt.Errorf("checkout branch failed (%s): %w", redactCredentials(stderr), err)

// setupUpstreamRemotes reconfigures remotes after cloning from upstream:
// renames origin→upstream, then adds origin pointing to GitRemoteURL.
// Note: the local branch still tracks upstream/<branch> after this.
// This is intentional — gitPush uses explicit refspec (origin HEAD:<branch>),
// not tracking-based push, so tracking config doesn't affect push behavior.
return fmt.Errorf("failed to rename origin to upstream (%s): %w", redactCredentials(stderr), err)
return fmt.Errorf("failed to add origin remote (%s): %w", redactCredentials(stderr), err)
